<!--
STUDENT NOTE: This policy exists so grading is fair and simple.
If you want credit, you must show links that prove the work happened.
-->

# POLICY — Evidence & Receipts

## How to use this policy
- When you do work, ask: **“What link proves it?”**
- Put evidence links in two places:
  1) The Weekly Sprint Packet Issue (team)
  2) Your personal receipt comment (individual)

**Rule:** If it isn’t linked, it didn’t happen.

## Purpose
This class runs like an engineering studio. That means we grade what we can **verify**.
Evidence is how you prove work happened and quality improved.

## What counts as evidence (good)
Use links to GitHub objects whenever possible:
- **Pull Request link** (best)
- **Issue link** (planning + tracking)
- **Code review link** (your review comments)
- **CI run / checks link** (proof it builds/tests)
- **Commit link** (only if tied to an Issue/PR)
- **Screenshot stored in the repo** (in `/docs/images/` or similar) + linked from an Issue/PR
- **Demo video link** (YouTube/unlisted/Drive) or live demo URL

## What does NOT count (usually)
- “I worked on it” with no links
- Screenshots pasted only into chat/LMS with no repo link
- A commit dump with no Issue/PR context
- Work on someone else’s machine with no trace

## Where evidence must appear
### 1) Weekly Sprint Packet (team submission)
The Sprint Packet Issue must include:
- demo link + script
- board snapshot + owners
- sprint notes (shipped, broke, next plan, risks)
- engineering practice spine + evidence link

### 2) Individual Contribution Receipts (each student)
Each student adds a **comment** on the Sprint Packet Issue with **2–3 links**:
- PRs you authored or meaningfully contributed to
- Issues you closed (or moved with clear actions)
- Reviews you wrote (link to review comment)
- CI runs you fixed (link)

## Examples
**Good receipt comment:**
- PR: Fix login validation + tests — (link)
- Issue: Wrote Week 3 demo script + backup plan — (link)
- Review: Reviewed teammate PR and requested changes — (link)

**Weak receipt comment:**
- “Helped with frontend and debugging this week.”

## Common mistakes (avoid these)
- Doing work but never opening an Issue
- Pushing directly to `main`
- “Big bang” PR the night before with no traceable steps
- Demo link missing or outdated

## Submission method
- Team submits **one** “Weekly Sprint Packet” Issue each week.
- Each student adds **one comment** on that Issue with receipts.
